methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Ultra Robotics, founded by Max Friefeld, is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $3M+. The valuation analysis draws on a broader comp universe of 11 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), though the Seed-stage segment lacks sufficient narrow peers for reliable median benchmarking.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier to the $3M total raised proxy, deriving an implied valuation of $7.5M. This multiplier reflects typical capital step-ups from Seed to Series A in the robotics sector. The Peer-Median approach was deemed inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics manufacturing universe—while 11 sector peers exist (including private firms like 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics), none met the threshold of 3+ datapoints in narrow valuation tiers. A reliance on all-sector medians would introduce material distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, with a median point of $8M. This range reflects 15% downside and upside variance around the stage-progression implied value. The primary uncertainty stems from the absence of tightly comparable Seed-stage data in robotics manufacturing; broader sector comps provide directional context but limited precision. Any valuation within this band should be treated as provisional pending additional comparable transactions or financial performance milestones.
